nativeRange.habitat: Cultivated dwarf trade form; the parent Bolbitis heteroclita 'Difformis' phenotype originates from the Philippines, where it grows as a small epiphytic fern on wet rocks and wood at the margins of shaded forest streams. Some vendors also market the 'Baby Leaf' trade name as an African Bolbitis heudelotii dwarf — provenance is not consistently reported across sources.

summary: A dwarf epiphytic Bolbitis fern with small Java-fern-like fronds, typically reaching only 5-10 cm; same care profile as its parent Bolbitis heteroclita — rhizome tied to driftwood or rock, low to moderate light, and CO2 optional.
careNotes: Mini Bolbitis 'Baby Leaf' is a smaller-growing trade form of the genus Bolbitis, most consistently identified as the Philippine-origin 'Difformis' cultivar of Bolbitis heteroclita (parent entry fw-plant-099), though some vendors market the same or a similar dwarf form as a small-leaf variant of the African Bolbitis heudelotii (fw-plant-009) — provenance and parent species are inconsistently reported across the hobby trade. Aquarium care is the same regardless: it is a slow-growing epiphytic rhizome fern, and the rhizome must be tied or glued to driftwood or porous rock and NEVER buried, as covering it reliably causes rot. Submerged fronds typically reach only 2-4 inches (about 5-10 cm), making it suitable for foreground-to-midground attachment in smaller tanks where the standard species would overgrow. Lighting preference is low to moderate (Buce Plant: low; Terrarium Master/Plant Addicts: 25-50 PAR, 6-8 h photoperiod); intense direct light can bleach the delicate fronds or trigger algae on the leaves. CO2 is not required (Buce: 'Not necessary'; Buce Plant Profile: 'will increase growth rate slightly') but does meaningfully speed growth. Soft to moderately hard, slightly acidic to neutral water is preferred; sources agree that parameter stability matters more than hitting exact numbers — sudden swings cause leaf melt. Place in moderate-to-higher flow areas; vendors specifically note that better flow helps prevent algae deposition on the fronds. Newly imported specimens often go through an acclimation 'melt' where fronds turn transparent and disintegrate before the plant produces new submerged-form leaves.
breedingNotes: Propagated vegetatively by dividing the horizontal rhizome with sharp sterile scissors. Each division should retain at least two to three growth points (fiddleheads) plus existing fronds and roots, and be tied or glued to a fresh piece of hardscape until it anchors naturally. The Buce Plant species profile recommends waiting until the parent plant has 3-4 leaves on each side of the rhizome before splitting. Adventitious plantlets occasionally appear on mature fronds and can be detached once they carry their own small rhizome and roots. Sexual reproduction by spores is limited to emersed-grown specimens and is essentially unreported for submerged aquarium culture.

primary.name: Buce Plant
primary.notes: Primary source for scientific identification (Bolbitis heteroclita 'Difformis'), origin (Southeast Asia), submerged size (2-4 inches / 5-10 cm), low lighting, pH (6-7.5), CO2 ('Not necessary'), epiphyte placement, rhizome-burial warning, propagation by rhizome division, and beginner difficulty. Same vendor's blog profile is internally inconsistent about parent species (heudelotii vs heteroclita); we follow the product page's 'heteroclita Difformis' identification as it matches the Philippine 'Difformis' cultivar of fw-plant-099.

additional.name: Terrarium Master — Mini Bolbitis (Baby Leaf)
additional.notes: Cross-checked temperature (68-77°F = 20-25°C), pH (5.5-7.5), soft water preference, lighting (low to moderate, 25-50 PAR, 6-8 h photoperiod), CO2 optional, epiphyte-on-hardscape placement, propagation by rhizome division (2-3 growth points per cut), water-column feeder (no root feeding), and 'melt' on introduction. Provides the broader pH floor (5.5) and lower temperature floor (20°C) used here.

additional.name: Buce Plant — Plant Profile: Bolbitis heudelotii 'Difformis'
additional.notes: Source of the temperature upper bound (70-80°F = 21-27°C, taken as 27°C max here) and confirmation of low-to-moderate light, 6-8 h photoperiod, soft-water preference, CO2-optional, midground/epiphyte placement, rhizome-never-buried, higher-flow placement to prevent algae, and water-parameter stability sensitivity. Note: this profile attributes the cultivar to Bolbitis heudelotii (Africa), while the same vendor's product page attributes it to Bolbitis heteroclita Difformis (Southeast Asia) — the trade name 'Mini Bolbitis Baby Leaf' is used for both lineages in the hobby.

additional.name: KeepFishKeeping — Mini Bolbitis (Baby Leaf)
additional.notes: Third source: identifies the product as genus Bolbitis 'Native To: Africa' (cultivated at Florida Aquatic Nurseries), with low-to-high light tolerance, slow growth, soft-water preference (tolerates moderate hardness), spreading-rhizome propagation, and epiphyte attachment to driftwood/rock. Used to confirm undemanding/beginner difficulty and the African-lineage trade interpretation noted in habitat.

additional.name: Wikipedia — Bolbitis
additional.notes: Taxonomy fallback: family Dryopteridaceae, order Polypodiales. Confirms that Bolbitis heudelotii normally grows fully submerged while Bolbitis heteroclita grows at water margins and tolerates submersion — relevant to the parent-species ambiguity for this dwarf trade form.

propagation: rhizome division (2-3 growth points per cut)
